title: Batch Trace Processor Run Loop
description: Background daemon thread loop in BatchTraceProcessor: collects spans/traces into batches, exports completed batches via TracingExporter, flushes all on shutdown.

initialization.thread_start: Lazy — thread started on first on_trace_start() or on_span_end() call

_ensure_thread_started: Fast path: if worker_thread is alive → return immediately
If still not alive → create daemon Thread(target=_run)

steps: Collect items from queue up to max_batch_size (or all items if force=True)
Handle queue.Empty exception (race condition between threads)
_export_batches_activity.notes: force=True ignores max_batch_size limit; force=False caps each batch at max_batch_size (128)

steps: Acquires _export_lock → waits for any in-flight background export to finish first
worker_thread.join(timeout=timeout)  [wait for _run to finish final drain]

queue_overflow_handling: on_trace_start: queue.Full → logger.warning('Queue is full, dropping trace.')
on_span_end:    queue.Full → logger.warning('Queue is full, dropping span.')

exporter_retry_logic.backoff: Exponential with 10% jitter, base_delay=1.0s, max_delay=30.0s, max_retries=3
exporter_retry_logic.grouping: Items grouped by tracing_api_key before export
exporter_retry_logic.sanitization: Input/output fields truncated to 100,000 bytes for OpenAI endpoint; usage keys filtered

threading_model.concurrency_note: force_flush() and _run() both call _export_batches(); _export_lock ensures mutual exclusion

global_singletons._global_processor: BatchTraceProcessor wrapping global exporter, lazily initialized

key_relationships: BatchTraceProcessor creates and owns background threading.Thread
important_notes: Traces enqueued on on_trace_start; Spans enqueued on on_span_end only (start is no-op)
Thread is daemon so process can exit without explicit shutdown
Long-running workers (Celery, FastAPI) should call flush_traces() after each job for immediate delivery
Tracing disabled via OPENAI_AGENTS_DISABLE_TRACING=1 or set_tracing_disabled(True)
